premises.content: In any topological space $(Y, \mathcal{T})$, for any collection of subsets $\{A_\alpha\}$, the union of closures is contained in the closure of the union: $\bigcup_{\alpha} \operatorname{cl}_Y(A_\alpha) \subseteq \operatorname{cl}_Y\!\left(\bigcup_{\alpha} A_\alpha\right)$.
premises.label: closure_union_containment

premises.content: In topological spaces, density is transitive: if $A$ is dense in subspace $B$ and $B$ is dense in ambient space $C$, then $A$ is dense in $C$.
premises.label: density_transitivity

premises.content: Every integer $n > 1$ can be expressed as a product of prime numbers (Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic). Equivalently, every integer greater than 1 is divisible by at least one prime, so $\bigcup_{p \in P} M_p = \mathbb{N} \setminus \{1\}$.
premises.label: fundamental_theorem_arithmetic

premises.content: For all positive integers $x, n, m$: $\gcd(x, n) = 1$ and $\gcd(x, m) = 1$ if and only if $\gcd(x, nm) = 1$. As a consequence, $\sigma_{nm} = \sigma_n \cap \sigma_m$ for all $n, m \in \mathbb{N}$.
premises.label: gcd_multiplicativity

premises.content: For every positive integer $n$, the set $\sigma_n = \{m \in \mathbb{N} : \gcd(n, m) = 1\}$ is infinite. For $n > 1$, $\sigma_n$ consists of all positive integers in the residue classes $m \pmod{n}$ with $\gcd(m, n) = 1$; each such arithmetic progression $\{m, m+n, m+2n, \ldots\}$ is infinite.
premises.label: sigma_n_infinite
